title: Journalist murder case: Court acquits Dera Sacha Sauda chief Gurmeet Ram Rahim
content: The Punjab and Haryana High Court has acquitted Gurmeet Ram Rahim in the journalist Ram Chander Chhatrapati murder case, overturning a 2019 life sentence. While the Dera Sacha Sauda chief was acquitted, three other accused had their convictions upheld. The court's decision came after an appeal challenging the CBI's investigation and evidence.

title: 'This is humiliation': Congress slams PM Modi's silence over US 'permission' on Russian oil
content: Congress party has strongly criticized Prime Minister Narendra Modi. The party stated the US gave India 'permission' to buy Russian oil. This move is seen as a humiliation for India. Congress demands an explanation from the Prime Minister. The US Treasury Secretary confirmed the permission to ease global oil supplies.

title: Gukesh apologises to fans; Indian stars slip in rankings: what’s going wrong?
content: Gukesh Dommaraju’s emotional apology during the Prague Masters highlighted a broader dip in form among India’s top players, including Arjun Erigaisi and Rameshbabu Praggnanandhaa. Experts cite heavy schedules, distractions from fame, repetitive elite events and overreliance on engine preparation as factors. It is also likely that the slump is temporary.

title: 'Imperative for us to lead by example': Infosys makes four days a week compulsory for more employees
content: Infosys is mandating junior employees (job level 6A and above) to work from the office at least four days a week, extending its previous policy. This move aims to reinforce hybrid work guidelines and improve collaboration, following similar shifts by rivals like Wipro and TCS. The company is reviewing monthly return-to-office data to ensure adherence.

title: Bhopal teacher dies after consuming bhang at Holi gathering with students
content: A 26-year-old teaching faculty member died under suspicious circumstances after allegedly consuming bhang during a Holi gathering with students. Krishna Nayak reportedly consumed multiple glasses of bhang, leading to him feeling unwell and later dying at a hospital. The exact cause of death is pending a post-mortem report.

title: 'That phase was very challenging': Samson makes honest admission
content: Sanju Samson has been one of India’s standout players in the World Cup, scoring 97* against West Indies and 89 against England in the semifinal. The 31-year-old credits his experience and clarity about his role for the performances. After years of inconsistent opportunities, Samson has finally made a strong impact and is now shortlisted for Player of the Tournament.

title: 'Indians been good actors': Why US agreed to let India buy Russian oil again
content: The US has granted India a temporary waiver to purchase Russian oil stranded at sea, aiming to stabilise global supplies amid West Asian conflict disruptions. This move, praised by US Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent, allows India to ease short-term supply pressures while maintaining its commitment to reducing sanctioned Russian oil imports.

title: Microsoft becomes first company to say it is not 'abandoning' Anthropic
content: Microsoft will continue to integrate Anthropic's AI models into its products, despite the Department of War designating the startup as a supply-chain risk. The company's legal team reviewed the designation and concluded Anthropic's offerings, excluding those for the Department of War, can remain available to customers.

title: 'It felt like small tremor': 21 injured as mosque balcony wall collapses in Jaipur
content: A balcony wall at Firdaus Masjid in Jaipur collapsed shortly after Friday prayers, injuring at least 21 people. The incident occurred around 2:30 pm as devotees were exiting the mosque. Preliminary investigations suggest structural issues, possibly exacerbated by worshippers leaning on the aging, single-brick structure.

title: Research company claims spyware posing as Israel's Red Alert Android app targeting users
content: A sophisticated cyberattack is targeting Israeli civilians, exploiting a trusted emergency app. Hackers are distributing a fake 'Red Alert' application, disguised as an update from the Home Front Command, to steal personal data. This trojanized app mimics the real version, sending genuine alerts while secretly harvesting messages, contacts, and location data, raising serious security concerns.

title: T20 WC: From trump card to major concern? Varun Chakravarthy losing grip at wrong time
content: With 13 wickets to his name, Varun Chakravarthy is currently the joint-leading wicket-taker of the 2026 T20 World Cup. In the first four matches, he picked up nine wickets at an economy rate of 5.17. In the last four games, however, he has leaked runs at 11.63 while picking up just four wickets. The mystery spinner is struggling with his lengths and has been taken to the cleaners, which is not a good sign for India ahead of the final against New Zealand.

title: Plumes of smoke, planes ablaze: Iran's Mehrabad airport on fire as Israel launches airstrike
content: Israeli forces launched broad-scale strikes on targets in Tehran, including Mehrabad International Airport, with explosions reported across the city and surrounding areas. This escalation follows Iran's retaliatory missile and drone attacks against Israel and Gulf countries, intensifying the ongoing conflict in West Asia.

title: Delhi's Uttam Nagar on edge after Holi killing: CRPF deployed amid heighted communal tensions
content: Two days after a fatal Holi dispute in Delhi's Uttam Nagar, a car and bike, allegedly belonging to an accused's relative, were torched, escalating tensions. Heavy security is now in place as police investigate the vehicle burning and have arrested eight individuals in connection with the killing.

title: US war with Iran: Trump announces quadruple production of 'exquisite weaponry'
content: Six major US defense contractors agreed to quadruple production of advanced weapons following a meeting with President Trump. This move aims to rapidly increase US stockpiles amid escalating Middle East tensions. Expansion efforts were already underway, with companies committed to strengthening American military capabilities.

title: Inside Bumrah’s constant evolution: An insatiable appetite for success
content: Jasprit Bumrah’s relentless training, including repeated yorker drills in practice, reflects his hunger to stay the best despite years of success and injuries. The 32-year-old pacer delivered another crucial performance in India’s World Cup semifinal against England, controlling the game with precise bowling. Praised by England captain Harry Brook, Bumrah continues proving why he remains India’s most reliable bowler.

title: Karnataka woman, lover held for smothering husband, staging his heart attack
content: A Bengaluru man, initially reported to have died of a heart attack, was actually smothered to death. Police exhumed his body, revealing the truth. His wife and her alleged lover have been arrested for the murder, orchestrated after the victim discovered their affair. The wife then falsely claimed a heart attack to conceal the crime.

title: 'Me bas fielding pe cricket nahi khela': Amir launches brutal attack on Kaif
content: Mohammad Amir and Mohammad Kaif are engaged in a verbal spat during the T20 World Cup 2026. Kaif accused Amir of seeking attention by criticizing India, while Amir retorted by questioning Kaif's career stats and suggesting he owes his success to Sourav Ganguly. Amir clarified his comments were opinions, not personal attacks.

title: Knicks vs Nuggets injury report, lineups, head-to-head and key stats
content: The Knicks, battling injuries to key guards like Miles McBride, face the Nuggets in Denver on March 6, 2026. Despite recent success against Denver, New York's backcourt depth is tested. The Nuggets, also missing frontcourt players Aaron Gordon and Peyton Watson, aim to leverage home-court advantage. This crucial matchup sees star big men Nikola Jokic and Karl-Anthony Towns go head-to-head.

title: Miami Heat vs Hornets injury report: Full list, H2H, team stats
content: The Miami Heat face the Charlotte Hornets on March 6, 2026, with playoff positioning on the line. Miami, holding a slight edge in the standings and season series, will be without key players like Terry Rozier and Norman Powell, impacting their rotation. Charlotte, seeking a seventh straight win, relies on strong home defense and perimeter shooting.

title: Taylor Swift-Travis Kelce wedding plans shaken by Matty Healy’s wedding
content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce have billions of fans around the world waiting for the two to tie the knot in the next few months. But it seems the billionaire pop star, Taylor Swift, might be facing a few strong emotions about her ex boyfriend, Matty Healy, after strong rumors suggest that he is also tying the knot in the next few months.

title: LPG price hike: Domestic, commercial cylinders to get costlier from today — report
content: Domestic cooking gas prices are set to rise from Saturday, with a 14.2-kg LPG cylinder increasing by Rs 60 nationwide. Commercial cylinders will also see a Rs 115 hike. This marks the first significant domestic LPG price change since April 2025. Despite global energy concerns, the government assures comfortable energy supply and diversified import sources.

title: 'Lost to Australia in 2023': Akhtar says Ahmedabad pressure will haunt India in T20 WC final
content: Former Pakistan cricketers Shoaib Akhtar and Umar Gul highlighted the immense pressure on India ahead of the T20 World Cup 2026 final against New Zealand.  They cited the weight of fan expectations and India's recent struggles at the Narendra Modi Stadium, including losses in major finals, as potential challenges for the home team.

title: Mic-drop mandate as Nepal grooves to rapper Balen’s beat
content: Nepal's political landscape has been dramatically reshaped as the Rastriya Swatantra Party, spearheaded by Balen Shah, achieved a stunning victory in the parliamentary elections. This generational shift has seen veteran leaders facing significant defeats, with the urban electorate overwhelmingly backing the new force. The results signal a powerful rejection of the old political order.

title: Russian athletes booed during Winter Paralympics opening ceremony
content: The Milan–Cortina Winter Paralympics opened in Italy on Friday but were overshadowed by controversy after Russian athletes marched under their national flag for the first time since 2014. The move drew boos and led several countries, including Ukraine and Poland, to boycott the ceremony. Over 600 athletes will compete in six sports until March 15. Officials emphasised peace, inclusion and solidarity.

title: Iran delays naming supreme leader out of security concerns: Officials
content: Iran has delayed naming a successor to its supreme leader due to security concerns, following threats from the US and Israel against potential candidates. Ayatollah Khamenei's son, Mojtaba, is a leading contender, but American and Israeli officials have publicly stated he would be an "unacceptable" target for elimination.

title: South Delhi home horror: Woman 'kills' 2 daughters, attempts suicide
content: In a shocking Malviya Nagar incident, police found two sisters dead and their mother unconscious. Authorities suspect the mother, Sunita, killed her daughters, Radhika and Gunisha, before attempting suicide. Crushed naphthalene balls and a crepe bandage were recovered from the scene. Investigations into potential depression and domestic discord are ongoing.

title: US DoJ releases missing interviews with woman who made claims against Donald Trump
content: The Justice Department has released previously withheld FBI interview notes concerning a woman's accusations of sexual assault against President Trump and Jeffrey Epstein. These documents, initially misclassified as duplicates, detail multiple interviews from 2019 about assaults dating back to the 1980s. Their delayed release has drawn criticism regarding the handling of Epstein-related files.

title: 'Headless batting in semi-final': R Ashwin’s brutal verdict on Abhishek Sharma
content: Ravichandran Ashwin backs young opener Abhishek Sharma for the T20 World Cup final despite his struggles, urging the team to persist with him. Ashwin, however, advised Abhishek to better understand match situations and avoid 'headless batting,' emphasizing timing over brute force. He stressed keeping the talented opener in a good mental space for the crucial clash.

title: Nitish Kumar firm on Rajya Sabha move, son Nishant to join JDU tomorrow
content: Nishant Kumar, son of Bihar CM Nitish Kumar, is set to join the JDU on March 8, marking his political debut. Nitish Kumar has affirmed his decision to move to the Rajya Sabha is final and irreversible, assuring party members he will remain involved in Bihar's development. Nishant's entry sparks speculation about his future role within the party or government.

title: 'Red corridor' will be crushed by March 31, says Amit Shah
content: Union Home Minister Amit Shah announced that left-wing extremism will be completely wiped out by March 31, ending the ambition of a "red corridor" from Tirupati to Pashupati. Speaking at CISF's raising day, he lauded security forces for curbing Naxal activities and launched housing projects for the paramilitary force.

title: US says India’s Russian oil waiver short-term step to stabilise global prices
content: The US has granted India a temporary waiver to buy Russian oil, aiming to stabilize global prices amid Middle East supply disruptions. This short-term measure allows India to access millions of barrels of Russian crude currently at sea, easing immediate pressure on refineries worldwide.

title: West Bengal: Didi begins sit-in, alleges EC 'plot' to delete voters' names
content: West Bengal CM Mamata Banerjee launched a sit-in protest, accusing the BJP and Election Commission of a conspiracy to disenfranchise voters by removing genuine names from electoral rolls. She highlighted instances of living individuals being declared dead, vowing to expose the alleged plot ahead of the EC's visit and upcoming assembly polls.

title: 'Jana Nayagan' may arrive in theatres on this date
content: Thalapathy Vijay's 'Jana Nayagan' is nearing its censor certification, with a screening scheduled for March 9, 2026. This development could pave the way for a release, potentially before the Tamil Nadu elections in late April or early May. The film's release was initially planned for Pongal but was delayed.

title: IAF pilot part of Operation Sindoor among 2 killed in Assam Sukhoi crash
content: Two IAF pilots, Squadron Leader Anuj Vashishth and Flight Lieutenant Purvesh Duragkar, were killed when their Su-30MKI jet crashed in Assam's Karbi Anglong district during a training sortie. The aircraft had taken off from Jorhat airbase and lost contact shortly after. Duragkar was known for his participation in Operation Sindoor last year.

title: MBBS graduate tops civil services exam in which professionals dominate
content: AIIMS graduate Anuj Agnihotri has clinched the top spot in the Civil Services Examination 2025, with 958 candidates recommended for various services. Medical, engineering, law, and commerce graduates once again dominated the prestigious exam. Rajeshwari Suve secured the second rank, followed by Ekansh Dhull at third.

title: Karnataka says it will ban social media for children under 16
content: Karnataka plans to ban social media for children under 16, aligning with global efforts to curb digital exposure. Chief Minister Siddaramaiah announced the policy, aiming to address concerns over screen addiction and mental health. While welcomed by educators, experts caution about potential FOMO and withdrawal, urging careful implementation and support for children.

title: Middle East conflict: There should be no cherry-picking on int'l law, says Iran dy FM
content: India's External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar met with Iranian Deputy Foreign Minister Saeed Khatibzadeh on the sidelines of Raisina Dialogue 2026. The meeting followed India's condolences over the killing of Iran's supreme leader. Khatibzadeh emphasized the importance of upholding international law against perceived atrocities and aggression.

title: Opposition notice to oust Speaker Om Birla to be taken up in Lok Sabha on Monday
content: Lok Sabha will debate a no-confidence motion against Speaker Om Birla on March 9, moved by Congress and allies over alleged partisan conduct. Despite NDA's majority rendering the motion's defeat inevitable, it will test opposition unity. Whips have been issued by both BJP and Congress for the crucial vote.

title: Manipur government committed to support railway authorities for completion of project connecting Imphal: CM
content: The railway line connecting Imphal to Jiribam town, bordering Assam's Cachar district, is a critical 111-km infrastructure project worth over ₹22,000 crore, which is expected to be fully operational by 2028

title: Dera Sacha Sauda chief Gurmeet Ram Rahim acquitted in journalist murder case
content: The court acquitted the Dera chief more than seven years after he was convicted and sentenced to life imprisonment

title: Decision on Iran strikes taken after Modi left Israel: Foreign Minister Sa'ar
content: “We could not brief Prime Minister Modi on this because the decision was taken only on Saturday early morning,” said Gideon Sa’ar

title: Karnataka Budget 2026: ₹4,291 crore earmarked to address regional imbalance
content: Experts have previously pointed out that successive governments have highlighted even usual development projects in backward regions, such as Kalyana Karnataka, as special development projects

title: Maharashtra to send 50 leopards to Reliance Foundation's Vantara facility in Gujarat: Sources
content: The Forest Department made the decision to strengthen conservation efforts for the big cats while also addressing concerns related to increasing human-leopard interactions in parts of Maharashtra

title: Haryana doctors announce OPD strike after assault by police officer
content: The association has demanded a registration of a case against Station House Officer, Gharaunda, Inspector Deepak, along with the other police personnel involved in the alleged assault, their immediate arrest and a time-bound probe into the matter.

title: Hyderabad wakes up to a layer of smog, IMD links it to construction activity
content: Telangana Pollution Control Board’s Sanathnagar pollution observatory was not functioning from 6 a.m. creating a gap in information and understanding about the nature of smog

title: CM Naidu calls for speedy execution of prioritised irrigation projects, especially Polavaram
content: CM Naidu prioritised quality construction, feedback from international experts and coordination with central government officials, in Polavaram project’s review meeting

title: Amit Shah holds meeting with Odisha's BJP MLAs, Rajya Sabha poll candidates
content: While the party has named Odisha BJP chief Manmohan Samal and MP Sujeet Kumar as its candidates, it backed former Union Minister Dilip Ray, who is contesting as an Independent nominee

title: Future will be much more multipolar, says External Affairs Minister Jaishankar
content: “Multipolarity is here to stay. What we will have... is to some extent, some of the bigger countries will make temporary compacts on limited issues,” he said

title: Russia will not disclose data on its crude export to India: Kremlin
content: Kremlin spokesman Dmitry Peskov's comments came hours after U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said that Washington has issued a temporary 30-day waiver to allow Indian refiners to purchase Russian oil

title: ‘We had asked India to stop buying sanctioned Russian oil this fall, they did,’ says U.S. Treasury Secretary Bessent
content: ‘To ease the temporary gap of oil around the world, we have given [India] permission to accept the Russian oil,’ he said

title: Protesters march demanding justice for victims of violence in Manipur
content: Valte, who had sustained injuries in a mob attack during the early period of ethnic violence in 2023, died on February 20 in a private hospital in Gurugram.

title: Use cooking fuel judiciously, consumers told
content: Hoteliers in Tamil Nadu fear distributors may cite shortages to divert supplies to the black market or increase prices, raising operating costs for restaurants and hotels

title: Having conversations on AI’s role in Wikipedia, says founder Jimmy Wales
content: Jimmy Wales says AI can contribute by making translations better but warns that “even a good machine translation” can fall short of expectations if “cultural questions” are not addressed

title: Domestic LPG price hiked by ₹60 amid West Asia conflict
content: Non-subsidised LPG, the one that common household users other than the Ujjwala beneficiaries use in their kitchens, will now cost ₹913 per 14.2 kg cylinder in Delhi, according to the Indian Oil Corporation (IOC) website; the price increase is effective from March 7

title: 204 of 238 Indian cities did not meet air quality standards: CREA
content: Ghaziabad was the most polluted city, recording an average PM2.5 concentration of 172 g/m³, followed by Noida (166 g/m³), and Delhi (163 g/m³), as per CREA, a Finland-based independent research group.

title: In a Bengal college, an Iftar party to be held by women for women
content: The event, called ‘2026 Dosti ki Iftar’ and being organised by the Deeniyat Muallima College (located in Santragachi, across the river from Kolkata) and the city-based initiative Know Your Neighbour, intends to “provide a space for women to exchanges their views, and break myths and stereotypes”.

title: Fadnavis presents ₹7.69 lakh crore Maharashtra Budget, loan waiver for farmers
content: The Budget proposes a ninefold growth of the agricultural economy, aiming to raise the sector’s GDP from $55 billion to $500 billion by 2047. The State’s revenue receipts are estimated at around ₹6.56 lakh crore, resulting in a revenue deficit of about ₹40,500 crore.

title: Andhra Pradesh firecracker unit blast: Gone in a flash fire
content: An explosion at a firecracker manufacturing unit in Vetlapalem village of Kakinada district, Andhra Pradesh, claimed 28 workers’ lives, including eight women. Preliminary investigations reveal that the unit’s owners had allegedly stored explosive materials far beyond the permitted limit and engaged 31 workers, nearly four times the number allowed under licence, in a bid to fulfil festival and wedding orders, reports T. Appala Naidu

title: Why does India require U.S. nod for securing its energy needs, ask Opposition leaders
content: The Opposition leaders question the government’s silence over U.S. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent’s latest remark objecting to the tone of his announcement ‘allowing’ India a temporary waiver to purchase Russian oil

title: U.S. waiver to procure Russian oil: Kerala flays Centre
content: It is outrageous that a country like India should appear to require approval from the U.S. for decisions such as oil imports, says Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan in a post on X

title: Youth, student groups criticise Karnataka Budget over jobs, education funding
content: Student and youth organisations have criticised the State Budget for 2026-27, alleging that it fails to address the pressing issues of unemployment among youth and the declining state of public education

title: YSRCP shirking responsibility of raising public issues in Assembly, says A.P. CM
content: The ₹3.32 lakh-crore budget has been approved in the Assembly, but a concerted effort is needed to spend every single rupee judiciously, says Naidu

title: Woman in Assam becomes first to get Indian citizenship after two years in detention camp
content: Dipali Das, who entered India in February 1988, was declared a non-citizen by the Foreigners’ Tribunal in Silchar

title: 56 Naan Mudhalvan scheme trainees clear civil services exam
content: All India Civil Services Coaching Centre, the institute of the State government, has registered an increase in results owing to Naan Mudhalvan Scheme.

title: Regulating internet is Centre’s domain, social media ban imposed by A.P., Karnataka may hit jurisdictional barrier
content: Recently, the Union Minister for Electronics and Information Technology Ashwini Vaishnaw said that the government was discussing age-based restrictions on social media use

title: DMK gearing up for massive show of strength in Tiruchi
content: Stalin is expected to kick off his election campaign at the ruling party’s 12th State conference at Siruganur on outskirts of the city; Minister K.N. Nehru is overseeing arrangements for the meet

title: Budget has increased debt burden, say BJP and JD (S)
content: In his pursuit of records,  Siddaramaiah has pushed the State’s economy in disarray: Union Minister Kumaraswamy

title: Pinarayi slams Centre’s ‘submission’ before US over strikes on Iran
content: The country being tied to imperial powers is a matter of concern, and strong public opinion should arise against it, says Chief Minister

title: Watch: Iranian warship strike near Sri Lanka raises questions for India
content: An Iranian warship was struck by a U.S. submarine near Sri Lanka, days after participating in India’s MILAN 2026 naval exercise. The incident raises major questions about India’s role in the Indian Ocean and the strategic balance between Washington and Tehran.

title: West Asia conflict: Airlines bring back 15,000 stranded passengers
content: As many as 51 flights are planned on Saturday (March 7, 2026)— the highest since airstrikes in the region beganon February 28, 2026

title: Budget reflects State’s continued focus on diverse growth: trade bodies
content: FKCCI welcomes several important initiatives aimed at strengthening the industrial ecosystem and creating new opportunities for investment, innovation, and employment

title: India granted emergency docking to Iranian naval ship in Kochi, days before attack on IRIS Dena
content: IRIS Lavan has been docked at Kochi since March 4 as the vessel had developed technical issues. “Its crew of 183 are currently accommodated at naval facilities in Kochi,” the source said

title: India must ask U.S. why it is targeting Iranian ships in Indian Ocean: Iran Minister Khatibzadeh
content: Iran’s Deputy Foreign Minister Saeed Khatibzadeh says his country is a “responsible power” and has not shut the Strait of Hormuz, he says the attack on Iran is “based on a lie” and pitches for diplomacy as the “only option”

title: Proposal to rename Victoria Hospital in Bengaluru takes doctors, patients by surprise
content: Doctors at the hospital say they had no prior information about the proposal until it was announced in the Budget

title: Karnataka Budget 2026–27: Capex slows as committed expenses balloon and revenue takes a hit
content: Karnataka pins hopes on raising its own tax revenue and increasing borrowing to push capital expenditure

title: A.P. liquor scam: ED attaches properties worth ₹441 crore of key accused
content: Assets belonging to Kesireddy Rajasekhara Reddy. Booneti Chanakya and Donthireddy Vasudeva Reddy attached under PMLA as probe points to alleged ₹3,500 crore loss to State exchequer during previous regime

title: SIR was conducted in a transparent manner, says CEC
content: Gyanesh Kumar quoted as saying in an official release that the objective of the SIR was to ensure that no eligible elector was left out and that no ineligible person was included in the electoral rolls

title: ₹2,000 crore electric bus programme with World Bank aid announced in Karnataka Budget 2026
content: The government also plans to induct 4,000 electric buses into the fleets of various State-run transport corporations as part of the initiative

title: Karnataka’s GSDP expected to grow at 8.10% against India’s GDP growth of 7.4%
content: While Bengaluru Urban has the highest per capita income at ₹8.55 lakh, Kalaburagi has the lowest with ₹1.44 lakh.

title: Trapped in toxicity: Residents of Visakhapatnam’s Gajuwaka struggle for breath of fresh air
content: The Garbage Transfer Station (GTS) located in Ward 76 of Gajuwaka amid densely populated colonies, has become a source of severe pollution and health issues. Locals complain of constant foul odour, black coal dust, mosquito menace, respiratory and skin ailments. Despite repeated agitations, the residents fear that long-term health consequences are imminent due to official apathy, explains Harish Gilai

title: Lok Sabha likely to debate resolution seeking Speaker Om Birla’s removal on March 9
content: Both the BJP and the Congress have issued whips mandating their members’ presence in the House from March 9 to 11, in anticipation of the resolution by 118 Opposition MPs in the Lok Sabha seeking Birla’s removal for partisan behaviour slated for debate

title: New Intermediate Ring Road connecting Tumakuru and Mysuru Roads, pedestrian push in Budget for Bengaluru
content: The State govt. has also allowed the new corporations to mobilise resources for development works through ‘municipal bonds’ based on their balance sheets

title: Nishant Kumar to join JD(U) on March 8, as Nitish promises to stick around
content: Nishant Kumar was not present at the emotional meeting of JD(U) leaders who offered him their “wholehearted” support; RJD’s sole RS candidate Amarendra Dhari Singh claims he has the votes to win

title: BRS faults Congress for giving full-page ads in Kerala with public money
content: Former Minister Harish Rao accuses Congress of funding a misleading poll campaign in Kerala with promises it failed to fulfil in Telangana

title: Karnataka Budget 2026: Aerospace common facility, flight training school to be set up
content: Siddaramaiah also announced that ₹200 crore has been earmarked for the development of seven domestic airports in the State

title: Watch: The Namakkal case that changed Indian elections forever
content: Can courts intervene when an election is already underway? In 1952, a rejected candidate from Namakkal took this question to the Supreme Court in the landmark N.P. Ponnuswami v. Returning Officer case.

title: New bodies for upliftment of deprived communities
content: In the year 2026-27, a total of ₹44,632 crore has been provided under Karnataka Scheduled Castes Sub-Plan (SCSP) and Tribal Sub-Plan (TSP)

title: Man gets life imprisonment in murder case
content: The accused beat the victim to death and took away Rs.750 from his pocket, said GRP CI J.V. Ramana, who investigated the case

title: CPI(M) flags anomalies in voter rolls, seeks clarity from election commission
content: M.V. Jayarajan cites the discrepancy between the voter rolls prepared by the State Election Commission and the final roll published by the Election Commission of India following the SIR to highlight the party’s concerns

title: I&B Ministry orders halt to TRP reporting of TV news channels for four weeks
content: The order has been issued in view of the “unwarranted sensationalism” in reporting the ongoing war in West Asia involving the U.S., Israel and Iran

title: Kerala shines in Civil Services exam; over 10 candidates in top 200
content: Sreeja J.S. from Naruvamoodu in Thiruvananthapuram secures 57th rank in her  first attempt, overcoming several oddsTHIRUVANANTHAPURAM

title: 9,000 posts in health sector filled in 20 months, says A.P. Minister
content: On an average, 450 vacancies have been filled every month under the government’s ‘zero vacancy’ policy aimed at strengthening healthcare facilities, says Satya Kumar Yadav

title: Mamata’s dharna in Kolkata is an attempt to disrupt SIR: West Bengal BJP
content: The BJP says that the Mamata Banerjee is abdicating her constitutional responsibility by conducting a dharna against the Election Commission of India

title: Andhra Pradesh Legislative Assembly adjourned sine die
content: House met for 15 days and conducted proceedings for 72 hours and 14 minutes, says Speaker Ayyanna Patrudu

title: Velur village to launch protest against removal of ‘St. Thomas’ from 103-year-old school’s name
content: Name changed after  school management handed it over to State government a few years ago. Demand to restore original name had been submitted to government and representations had been sent to Education Minister. Despite these efforts, no response was received from authorities so far

title: Learn new skills to thrive amid AI driven job shifts: JNTU GV Vice-Chancellor
content: Students must focus on emerging technology courses and industry relevant skills as recruitment strategies evolve in the era of Artificial Intelligence, he says

title: Sources: Ravens to acquire Crosby in blockbuster
content: The Ravens have won the Maxx Crosby sweepstakes, beating out the Cowboys and others by sending 2026 and 2027 first-round picks to the Raiders for the star pass rusher, sources told ESPN's Adam Schefter.

title: Trump plans 'all-encompassing' college sports EO
content: President Donald Trump on Friday said he will write an executive order within a week that will "solve all of the problems" brought forth in an unprecedented meeting at the White House to address the future of college sports.

title: Source: Patriots trading center Bradbury to Bears
content: The Bears found a new starting center, acquiring Garrett Bradbury from the Patriots in exchange for a 2027 fifth-round pick, a source told ESPN.

title: Ralph tossed as Vandy falls flat in SEC quarters
content: No. 5 Vanderbilt's slim hopes for a No. 1 seed in the NCAA tournament all but ended with Friday night's 89-78 defeat to Ole Miss, a loss that included head coach Shea Ralph getting ejected for arguing with officials in the fourth quarter.

title: Fired Michigan coach Moore reaches plea deal
content: Fired Michigan football coach Sherrone Moore pleaded no contest to two misdemeanors to resolve a felony home invasion case. The deal was struck on the same day that a judge planned to hear a challenge to Moore's arrest in December.

title: Penguins' Malkin banned 5 games for Dahlin slash
content: Pittsburgh forward Evgeni Malkin has been suspended five games for slashing Buffalo defenseman Rasmus Dahlin during Thursday's game between the Penguins and Sabres.

title: Texans grant Mixon release after lost '25 season
content: The Texans released veteran running back Joe Mixon on Friday after he missed the 2025 season with a foot injury.

title: Stewart: Union letter not to undermine Jackson
content: WNBPA vice president Breanna Stewart said Friday that the letter to players' union executive director Terri Jackson earlier this week was intended to "get the entire [executive committee] back on track" to get the best CBA possible.

title: ✍️ NHL trade deadline winners and losers
content: The final week was a fun one, with some late shockers. Here's who feels great now -- and who might not.

title: Maxx Crosby to the Ravens: What's next for both teams, plus free agency and draft implications
content: Is Baltimore a contender? What's next for Las Vegas? Answering six questions about the Maxx Crosby trade.

content: Atul Mathur is a Senior Assistant Editor at The Times of India with over 27 years of experience in journalism. Based in Delhi, he has spent much of his career reporting on governance, public policy and politics, churning out researched, data-driven stories that impact daily lives. Atul is known for investigative depth and strong human-interest narratives as he strives to bring clarity and context to complex issues. He currently tracks the energy sector, writing on power, renewable energy, coal and mines.
